reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict was based on a false positive regarding WaveLogGate.sh. The script executes a package-managed binary in /opt (root-owned post-installation), not a mutable user directory. All other code shows standard application behavior with no malicious patterns.
disagreement_analysis: qwen mischaracterized /opt/WaveLogGate/build/bin as mutable - in reality, package-managed system directories are root-controlled and not user-writable by default.
false_positives: WaveLogGate.sh 'unsafe' verdict: Fails to recognize Arch package management enforces root ownership of /opt subdirs, making binary replacement non-trivial without privilege escalation.
learnings: Improve context awareness: Audit tools should better model package installation context (file ownership/permissions)
Prioritize understanding of system directory write protections during script analysis
Enhance differentiation between user-writable vs package-managed paths in security assessments
